stories.headline: Trump says ceasefire is 'over' after US and Iran trade strikes

stories.headline: Trump threatens Spain trade, demands US take over Greenland at NATO summit

stories.headline: Top Democrats press Maine Senate candidate to drop out of race over sexual assault allegation

stories.headline: US Stocks Slide After Trump Says Iran Ceasefire Is Over
stories.summary: US equities fell as investors turned risk averse after President Donald Trump said the tentative ceasefire with Iran has ended, fueling concerns of a renewal in fighting.

stories.headline: US to give Ukraine licence to produce Patriot air-defence interceptors, Trump says

stories.headline: These Maine Democrats Might Replace Graham Platner in the Senate Race

stories.headline: Gaza aid worker killed minutes before World Cup screening he organised

stories.headline: UK and Norway leaders don national team shirts ahead of World Cup clash
stories.summary: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer and his Norwegian counterpart, Jonas Gahr Støre, held a bilateral meeting ahead of the NATO Summit in Ankara wearing their national team shirts. Both leaders backed their teams ahead of Friday's World Cup quarter-final in Miami.

stories.headline: Cubans protest after third nationwide power cut this year
stories.summary: While Cuban officials said that power had been mostly restored, those still left in the dark vented their anger.

stories.headline: Estonia Willing to Contribute to Free Passage in Hormuz, PM Says
stories.summary: Estonia's Prime Minister Kristen Michal said the NATO summit in Ankara, Turkey, has been a positive one. He also said his country would be willing to contribute to efforts securing free passage through the Strait of Hormuz. Michal spoke to Bloomberg's Oliver Crook on the sidelines of the summit. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Democratic factions vie to pick Platner replacement before he leaves Maine race

stories.summary: Argentina is set to make a major payment on its dollar bonds this week, pulling off a feat few investors thought possible while the country refuses to tap global debt markets.

stories.headline: Premier League clubs' pre-tax losses surged more than 600% in a single season
stories.summary: English top-flight football clubs saw their combined pre-tax losses balloon from £135 million (€158mn) to £948 million (€1.1bn) in the 2024/25 season, even as their revenues climbed to a record high.

stories.headline: Spanish strawberries among Europe's cleanest, study finds low pesticide levels
stories.summary: A study by PAN Europe and Ecologistas en Acción has found residues on 88% of strawberries tested in Europe, compared with a far lower rate in Spanish samples.
